invariant: Lighthouse should reflect a reviewed performance baseline.
Security and traceability contracts must remain valid.
forbidden_moves: Do not sacrifice readable source evidence just to shrink JavaScript transfer size.

candidate.description: Minify PWA runtime files to reduce Lighthouse total_js_bytes.

expected_effect: Lighthouse total_js_bytes should drop below the regression threshold.

green: Adversarial browser contract green after offline/Trusted Types fixes.
red: Lighthouse performance contract red on total_js_bytes.
Security contract failed because source-shape expectations were broken.
Feature traceability failed because evidence fragments disappeared.

orientation_center.reason: The transition tried to fix one metric but broke higher-priority source-governance invariants.
orientation_center.next_allowed_move: Restore readable runtime and solve Lighthouse through evidence-backed baseline refresh.

pattern: A red metric can tempt a local fix that harms global governance.
anomaly: Minification did not solve the actual Lighthouse governance issue.
risk: Future metric fixes may bypass traceability unless checked first.

rule_update: Reject source-shape changes when evidence contracts depend on exact tokens unless the contracts are intentionally updated too.
threshold_update: A baseline refresh must cite CI artifact evidence and preserve hard assertions.

project_invariant_alignment.reason: It aimed at Lighthouse, but not at the broader governance invariant.

side_effect_safety.reason: It broke Security and TRACE source-shape expectations.

evidence_path.reason: Lighthouse could check it, but source-governance impact was not safely covered.

exact_head_confidence.reason: CI ran on the exact head.
